deck.title: Onboarding Team Sync
deck.tagline: Weekly review · ship updates, experiment health, and what's next.

headlineFinding.kicker: Flow B is catching its target population
headlineFinding.explanation: matched a temporary-password account — invited barbers we want to catch before they hit Sign Up by mistake.
headlineFinding.takeaway: Without Flow B's email-first routing, this cohort would most likely have created accidental Indie accounts.

rollout.interpretation: First 6 days were at ≤20% rollout. Today is the first day at 50% — meaningful statistical reads start accumulating from tomorrow forward.

metrics.title: Variant balance — should be 50/50
metrics.definition: Distinct devices assigned to each variant — LD splits traffic, we measure with LOGIN_EXPERIMENT_ASSIGNED.
metrics.whyItMatters: If the split is uneven, every comparison downstream is biased.

currentRead.meaning: Both sources agree there's a real imbalance — ~13 percentage points off the expected 50/50.
currentRead.conclusion: ⚠️ Real SRM. Not invalidating yet — but we keep this in mind when comparing variants. Investigation in flight (Santi + Tristan).

metrics.title: Email check — who actually enters Flow B?
metrics.definition: When a Flow B user types their email, we hit the API and learn: are they a returning user, an invited barber with temp password, or a new email?
metrics.whyItMatters: This is the routing decision Flow B was designed to extract — and the experiment's whole reason to exist.

currentRead.data: 33.5% temp_password · 43.7% returning · 14.1% new_user · ~9% legacy username inputs. (382 events total, Flow B only.)
currentRead.meaning: 1 in 3 Flow B users is an invited barber we needed to catch BEFORE they hit Sign Up.
currentRead.conclusion: ✅ Flow B is doing exactly what it was designed to do at the routing level.

metrics.title: Login completion funnel by variant
metrics.definition: Distinct devices at each step — assigned → welcome viewed → login succeeded. Consistent ANONYMOUS_ID throughout.
metrics.whyItMatters: Tells us whether either flow breaks login for real users.

currentRead.data: Flow A: 418 → 358 → 210 · welcome 85.6% · conversion 58.7%. Flow B: 244 → 243 → 164 · welcome 99.6% · conversion 67.5%.
currentRead.meaning: Flow B has near-100% welcome rate (the email-first sheet works) AND ~9pp better login conversion than Flow A.
currentRead.conclusion: ✅ Email-first does NOT break login — it improves it. Strong guardrail signal.

metrics.title: Friction signals — where users struggle
metrics.definition: Counts of failed login attempts, forgot-password taps, and screen abandonment — per variant.
metrics.whyItMatters: Surfaces UX problems that completion rates alone miss.

currentRead.data: Abandonment: Flow A 202 (password step) · Flow B 180 (123 password + 57 NEW email step). Forgot password: A 196 · B 59. Login failed: A 0 · B 14.
currentRead.meaning: Flow B introduces an extra abandonment surface (the email step Flow A doesn't have). Login_failed Flow A absence is suspicious — older app versions emit the legacy event without the variant property.
currentRead.conclusion: 🟡 Friction is comparable, with one new surface in B (email step) to watch. Login_failed audit pending.

metrics.eyebrow: North star · instrumentation gap, not a null result
metrics.title: Reading next week — here's why
metrics.definition: ACCOUNT_CREATED where account_type='indie' AND came_from='signup_link', segmented by variant. The metric the experiment exists to move.
metrics.whyItMatters: If it drops in Flow B vs Flow A (or vs pre-experiment baseline), we shipped the right thing.

currentRead.data: Zero indie signups in either variant this window. Closest proxy (barber-type accidentals): Flow A 38 via signup_link · Flow B 35 via email_not_found.
currentRead.meaning: Either organic indie volume is low, or the indie account_type isn't being emitted on account creation. Tomorrow at 100% rollout we'll see if the zero holds.
currentRead.conclusion: 🚧 Need full-rollout traffic + property emission validation. Reading next week.

perExposure.title: Per-exposure rates tell a different story
perExposure.subtitle: Counts only barber-type signups (the original problem). We call them accidental because the population SHOULD be invited barbers logging in with credentials a shop owner already created — not creating fresh accounts. Per-exposure normalizes by total devices in each variant; Flow B's rate is ~57% higher.

flowA.label: Flow A · accidental barber via signup_link
flowA.rateLabel: of Flow A devices created an accidental barber-type account
flowA.path: User sees Sign Up button → taps it → signs up by mistake.

flowB.label: Flow B · accidental barber via email_not_found
flowB.rateLabel: of Flow B devices created an accidental barber-type account
flowB.path: User types email → API returns not-found → auto-redirects to signup.

perExposure.honestTake: Counts ONLY accidental barber-type signups (not total signups). Not declaring a winner — volume small, 7 days at ramp <50% most of it. Worth understanding before the experiment matures.

caveats.title: Indie account_type may not be emitting 🚧
caveats.detail: Both variants show zero indie signups this window. Either organic indie volume was low, or the indie property isn't being set when an Indie account is created.
caveats.fix: QA validates the firing path next week. Until then we use barber/signup_link as a proxy.

caveats.title: Login attempt failed: Flow A returns 0 events 🔍
caveats.detail: Suspicious. Likely older app versions emit the legacy 'login_failed' event without the variant property, so they get filtered out. The renamed event 'Login attempt failed' hasn't fully ramped.
caveats.fix: Audit which app versions emit which event. Cross-check LOGIN_ATTEMPT_FAILED next pull.

caveats.title: Sample Ratio Mismatch (63/37 instead of 50/50) ⚠️
caveats.detail: LD assigns 50/50; Snowflake observes 63/37. Confirmed source-of-truth deviation. Doesn't invalidate the headline read but biases raw count comparisons.
caveats.fix: Tristan + Santi investigating this week. Suspects: bucketing logic, cached pre-experiment exposures, non-uniform LD evaluation.

retrospective.context: The Onboarding squad is a small-team experiment within Squire — built to ship fast and iterate. This first project is the proof.

wentWell.title: Cross-platform parity at the wire level 🔁
wentWell.detail: iOS and Android emit identical event names + property values byte-for-byte. One Snowflake query covers both.

wentWell.title: Phased rollout managed risk 🛡️
wentWell.detail: 1% → 50% over 6 days, 100% tomorrow. No production incidents during the ramp.

wentWell.title: Analytics plan locked before code shipped 📋
wentWell.detail: Aligned with Tristan on north star + 9 events + threshold + caveats — guards against rationalizing post-hoc.

wentWell.title: Snowflake unblock → actionable data same day ⚡
wentWell.detail: Went from 'waiting for access' to 'first headline finding' within a single working session.

slowedDown.title: Unknowns surfaced during QA, not before 🧪
slowedDown.detail: Khem flagged username support; iOS auto sign-in wasn't tested → late fix.
slowedDown.learning: Pre-QA spike to enumerate edge cases before implementation locks in.

slowedDown.title: Concurrent feature-flag tooling migration 🔀
slowedDown.detail: LD → PostHog → GrowthBook in motion at the same time. Source-of-truth confusion.
slowedDown.learning: Lock the analytics destination before instrumenting.

slowedDown.title: Cross-platform context switching 📱
slowedDown.detail: iOS + Android both ramp-up territory. Ship work doubles when you're not the expert on either.
slowedDown.learning: Future onboarding work moves to React Native — familiar territory.

slowedDown.title: Access provisioning not pre-staged 🔑
slowedDown.detail: Snowflake + flag service accesses surfaced AFTER ship, delaying the first analytics pull.
slowedDown.learning: File access requests on day 1 of project.

commitmentDecisions.text: Phased Courses prototyping with Tristan — one course at a time, iterate with team
commitmentDecisions.ask: Starting next week. Flag if this conflicts with anything.

commitmentDecisions.text: Build login flows (A and B) in the new RN app in parallel with Appointments team
commitmentDecisions.ask: Starting in 2 weeks. When the winner is decided, RN is ready to ship.

commitmentDecisions.text: Coordinate Courses delivery with the RN launch (29 May)
commitmentDecisions.ask: Need clarity from Eric/Yurii: full feature parity, or onboarding-only first?

commitmentFyi: Investigate SRM root cause with Tristan — Snowflake confirms 63/37 device split
Audit signup_link_tapped scope leak (3 stray Flow B events) and login_failed asymmetry
Identify privacy team owner for user_id property sign-off
Continue weekly analytics extraction — fresh data each Monday in this deck

courses.title: Courses · the next chapter
courses.context: Indie onboarding restructured around course-based education. Already kicked off pre-experiment — paused during Tristan's vacation. Resuming this week.

tools.name: Claude Design
tools.note: Anthropic · 2-pane chat + live canvas. Reads Figma to extract design system.

tools.name: DESIGN.md
tools.note: Open-sourced spec by Stitch · brand rules consumed by every compatible agent.

tools.name: OpenPencil
tools.note: MIT · reads .fig natively, MCP server, design-to-code as JSX/Tailwind.

ourPlan: Take Tristan's existing Figma as the starting point
Drive prototypes through Claude Design + DESIGN.md spec for brand consistency
Iterate one course at a time — validate, refine, then move to code
Coordinate timing with the RN launch so it lands as a unit

tldr.eyebrow: Eric's question · our answer this week

highlights.text: Flow B catches its target — 33.5% of email checks are invited barbers
Flow B converts BETTER — 67.5% login completion vs 58.7% in Flow A
Per-device accidental rate is higher in Flow B (14.3% vs 9.1%) — small sample at <50% rollout, worth understanding
Real Sample Ratio Mismatch (63/37 instead of 50/50) — root cause in flight
